objective: Repair Local CI Gate enforcement wiring, portable shim behavior, and session-compliance evidence before any further consumer rollout.
scope_boundary: Stop consumer rollout work until this remediation is planned and implemented.
Record retrospective audit findings for Local CI Gate issues #265, #270, and #272.
Plan portable shim fixes so committed consumer shims do not embed absolute consumer worktree paths.
Plan live enforcement tests that prove blocker failures exit non-zero through an installed shim.
Plan governance profile execution-scope handling so it no longer hardcodes issue #253.
Plan runbook language that distinguishes profile-only, shim-installed, manual live gate, pre-push hook gate, and CI-required gate.
out_of_scope: Installing or refreshing knocktracker, AIS, or LAM consumer shims in this issue.
Editing dirty shared checkouts outside this isolated governance worktree.
Changing Local CI Gate profile command coverage except where required for the remediation.
Claiming hardgate status before a required invocation path is visibly wired and tested.
Closing retrospective compliance gaps without a recorded independent review artifact.
research_summary: Two read-only specialist reviews found that Local CI Gate profile dry-runs and profile availability were treated as stronger evidence than they are. The runner can fail blocker checks when invoked, but the hardgate path is not yet visibly wired as a required governance check, generated consumer shims can embed absolute target worktree paths, the governance profile references an old #253 execution scope, and closeout evidence did not sufficiently distinguish mapping proof from live enforcement proof.

sprints.name: Planning and Audit Record
sprints.goal: Land trusted planning and scope artifacts before any remediation implementation.
tasks: Add PDCA/R and planning cross-review artifacts using the specialist audit/debug findings.
Add planning and implementation execution scopes.
Update governance mirrors to show #275 as the active Local CI Gate remediation lane.
Validate structured plans, backlog alignment, diff hygiene, dual-signature metadata, and planner-boundary scope.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub issue #275 is open and referenced by governance mirrors.
Planning PR contains only planning/scope/cross-review/status artifacts.
Implementation branch and allowed write paths are declared before implementation starts.
Consumer rollout remains explicitly frozen until P0 remediation is complete.

sprints.name: Portable Shim and Enforcement Evidence
sprints.goal: Make the Local CI Gate deployer and evidence model match the actual enforcement contract.
tasks: Change generated shims so target repo root and shim path are resolved at runtime.
Keep `HLDPRO_GOVERNANCE_ROOT` for governance root override while removing embedded consumer worktree paths.
Add deployer tests proving a shim run from a copied/clean repo targets that repo.
Add live shim failure test proving blocker failures exit non-zero through the shim.
Add runner report fields for governance root/ref and shim path when provided.
acceptance_criteria: Generated shim body contains no absolute consumer repo path.
Installed shim run from a second clean repo targets that second repo.
A forced blocker failure exits non-zero through the shim.
Dry-run evidence is labeled mapping-only and cannot satisfy live-enforcement acceptance criteria.

sprints.name: Governance Scope and Status Taxonomy
sprints.goal: Remove stale governance-profile scope wiring and make runbook/closeout language precise.
tasks: Replace the hardcoded #253 execution scope in the governance profile with active issue-scope discovery or explicit scope override support.
Add tests for active branch issue scope resolution or explicit scope selection.
Update the Local CI Gate runbook with status taxonomy: profile-only, shim-installed, manual live gate, pre-push hook gate, CI-required gate.
Add retrospective audit artifact for #268/#269/#273/#274 session deviations.
Update closeout evidence expectations so exact PR check names and scope assertion status are recorded.
acceptance_criteria: Governance profile no longer hardcodes issue #253 execution scope.
Runbook states AIS and LAM are profile-only until their consumer rollout PRs land.
Knocktracker is marked as needing portable-shim refresh until a separate consumer PR fixes it.
Retrospective audit records self-approval, branch naming, dry-run evidence, and completion-protocol deviations.

specialist_reviews.focus: Audit session conduct against repo workflow rules and user instructions.
specialist_reviews.summary: The session collapsed planner, implementer, reviewer, merger, and closer roles; overstated hardgate coverage; normalized failed local scope assertions; used disallowed feat/ branch prefixes; and skipped adequately evidenced verify-completion.

specialist_reviews.focus: Review Local CI Gate deployer, runner, profiles, and consumer wiring for hardgating gaps.
specialist_reviews.summary: The current toolkit is profile-ready but not fully enforcement-ready. Generated shims can embed stale consumer worktree paths, Local CI Gate is not visibly wired as a required hardgate, the governance profile has stale #253 scope wiring, and dry-run evidence was overused.

alternate_model_review.summary: Operator requested audit and debug-specialist review, then approved moving into a planning-only remediation issue. Implementation remains blocked until this planning PR is reviewed and merged.
evidence: User request: spawn audit agent and debug specialist, present for review

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #275 may remediate Local CI Gate portable shim generation, live enforcement tests, governance execution-scope handling, evidence taxonomy docs, retrospective audit artifacts, and closeout evidence. It must not modify consumer repos.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge this planning/scope branch so the implementation execution scope is trusted from main, then implement on branch feature/issue-275-local-ci-enforcement-remediation.
blocked_on: planning PR merged so implementation scope is trusted from main

material_deviation_rules: If implementation needs consumer repo changes, stop and open the relevant consumer issue.
If hardgate wiring requires branch protection or GitHub ruleset changes outside repo files, record the blocker and do not claim completion.
If local scope assertion fails, classify it as locally blocked or rerun from a clean verification root; do not call it pass-equivalent.
If independent review cannot be evidenced, do not merge implementation.
